components.description: Signal to metrics connector produces metrics from all signal types (traces, logs, or metrics).
components.name: signaltometricsconnector

components.description: This extension allows acking of data upon successful processing. The upstream agent can choose to send event again if ack fails.
components.name: ackextension

components.description: This extension provides [Atlassian Service Authentication Protocol](https://s2sauth.bitbucket.io/) (ASAP) client credentials for HTTP or gRPC based exporters.
components.name: asapauthextension

components.description: The AWS proxy accepts requests without any authentication of AWS signatures applied and forwards them to the AWS API, applying authentication and signing. This allows applications to avoid needing AWS credentials to access a service, instead configuring the AWS exporter and/or proxy in the OpenTelemetry collector and only providing the collector with credentials.
components.name: awsproxy

components.description: The `avrolog` encoding extension is used to unmarshal AVRO and insert it into the body of a log record. Marshalling is not supported.
components.name: avrologencodingextension

components.description: This extension unmarshalls metrics encoded in formats produced by [Amazon CloudWatch Metric Streams](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/CloudWatch-Metric-Streams.html).
components.display_name: AWS CloudWatch Metric Streams Encoding Extension
components.name: awscloudwatchmetricstreamsencodingextension

components.description: This extension unmarshalls logs encoded in formats produced by AWS services.
components.name: awslogsencodingextension

components.description: This extension is designed for unmarshaling logs/traces/metrics encoded in specific format produced by [Azure Diagnostic Settings Export](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings) or [Azure Data Collection Rules (DCRs)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/data-collection/data-collection-rule-overview)
components.name: azureencodingextension

components.description: This extension can be used to unmarshall a [Cloud Logging LogEntry](https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/v2/rest/v2/LogEntry) message type.
components.name: googlecloudlogentryencodingextension

components.description: This extension unmarshals and marshals data encoded according to the [OTLP specification](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otlp/).
components.name: otlpencodingextension

components.description: The `Skywalking` encoding extension is used to unmarshal Apache Skywalking segment traces. Marshalling is not supported.
components.name: skywalkingencodingextension

components.description: The `text_encoding` extension is an encoding extension that can unmarshal and marshal logs.
components.name: textencodingextension

components.description: This extension supports marshaling and unmarshalling Zipkin data representing traces.
components.name: zipkinencodingextension

components.description: This extension provides Google OAuth2 Client Credentials and Metadata for gRPC and http based exporters.
components.name: googleclientauthextension

components.description: This extension accepts HTTP requests, optionally adds headers to them and forwards them. The RequestURIs of the original requests are preserved by the extension.
components.name: httpforwarderextension

components.description: This extension enables OpenTelemetry components to run in HA mode across a Kubernetes cluster. The component that owns the lease becomes the leader and becomes the active instance.
components.name: k8sleaderelector

components.description: This extension provides Sigv4 authentication for making requests to AWS services. You can read about the [Sigv4 process](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/signature-version-4.html).
components.name: sigv4authextension

components.description: The Database Storage Extension can persist state to a relational database.
components.name: dbstorage

components.description: The File Storage Extension can persist state to the local file system.
components.name: filestorage

components.description: The Redis Storage Extension can persist state to a Redis cluster.
components.name: redisstorageextension

components.description: The Coralogix processor adds attributes to spans that enable features in Coralogix.
components.name: coralogixprocessor

components.description: The cumulative to delta processor (`cumulativetodeltaprocessor`) converts monotonic sum, histogram, and exponential histogram metrics from cumulative to delta aggregation temporality. Non-monotonic sums are excluded. Delta metrics are excluded from any conversion and forwarded without changes.
components.name: cumulativetodeltaprocessor

components.description: The Delta to Cumulative Processor (`deltatocumulativeprocessor`) converts metrics from delta temporality to cumulative, by accumulating samples in memory.
components.name: deltatocumulativeprocessor

components.description: The Delta to Rate Processor (`deltatorateprocessor`) converts delta sum metrics to rate metrics. This rate is a gauge.
components.name: deltatorateprocessor

components.description: The Drain Processor applies the Drain log clustering algorithm to log records as they pass through the pipeline, deriving a template string (e.g. "user <*> logged in from <*>") and attaching it as an attribute on each record.
components.name: drainprocessor

components.description: The Filter Processor allows dropping spans, span events, metrics, datapoints, and logs from the collector.
components.name: filterprocessor

components.description: The geoIP processor `geoipprocessor` enhances the attributes of a span, log, or metric by appending information about the geographical location of an IP address.
components.name: geoipprocessor

components.description: This processor re-associates spans, log records and metric datapoints to a *Resource* that matches with the specified attributes. As a result, all spans, log records or metric datapoints with the same values for the specified attributes are "grouped" under the same *Resource*.
components.name: groupbyattrsprocessor

components.description: The **Isolation Forest processor** adds inline, unsupervised anomaly detection to any OpenTelemetry Collector pipeline (traces, metrics, or logs). It embeds a lightweight implementation of the Isolation Forest algorithm that automatically learns normal behaviour from recent telemetry and tags, scores, or optionally drops anomalies *in‑flight* – no external ML service required.
components.name: isolationforestprocessor

components.description: The Kubernetes Attributes Processor allows adding k8s metadata to resource attributes for spans, metrics and logs.
components.name: k8sattributesprocessor

components.description: The Log DeDuplication Processor is used to deduplicate logs by detecting identical logs over a range of time and emitting a single log with the count of logs that were deduplicated.
components.name: logdedupprocessor

components.description: The lookup processor enriches telemetry signals by performing external lookups to retrieve additional data. It evaluates an [OTTL](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/blob/main/pkg/ottl/README.md) value expression to extract a lookup key, queries a lookup source, and writes the results as new attributes. Currently supports logs, with metrics and traces support planned.
components.name: lookupprocessor

components.description: The Metric Start Time Processor (`metric_start_time`) is used to set the start time for metric points with a cumulative aggregation temporality. It is commonly used with the `prometheus` receiver, which usually produces metric points without a start time.
components.name: metricstarttimeprocessor

components.description: This processor deletes span, log, and metric datapoint attributes that don't match a list of allowed attributes. It also masks attribute values that match a blocked value list. Attributes that aren't on the allowed list are removed before any value checks are done.
components.name: redactionprocessor

components.description: The Unroll Processor takes log records with slice bodies and expands each element of the slice into its own log record. This allows for better processing and analysis of structured log data that contains arrays or lists.
components.name: unrollprocessor

components.description: The Active Directory Domain Services Receiver scrapes metrics relating to an Active Directory domain controller using the Windows Performance Counters.
components.name: activedirectorydsreceiver

components.description: The Aerospike receiver is designed to collect performance metrics from one or more Aerospike nodes.
components.name: aerospikereceiver

components.description: The Apache Web Server Receiver fetches stats from an Apache Web Server instance using the `server-status?auto` endpoint.
components.name: apachereceiver

components.description: The Apache Spark Receiver fetches metrics for an Apache Spark cluster through the Apache Spark REST API - specifically, the `/metrics/json`, `/api/v1/applications/[app-id]/stages`, `/api/v1/applications/[app-id]/executors`, and `/api/v1/applications/[app-id]/jobs` endpoints.
components.name: apachesparkreceiver

components.description: The AWS CloudWatch Receiver receives Cloudwatch events from [AWS CloudWatch](https://aws.amazon.com/cloudwatch/) via the [AWS SDK for Cloudwatch Logs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-go/api/service/cloudwatchlogs/)
components.name: awscloudwatchreceiver

components.description: The AWS Kinesis Data Firehose Receiver is for ingesting delivery stream messages and parsing the records received based on the configured record type.
components.name: awsfirehosereceiver

components.description: The Azure Monitor Receiver scrapes the Azure Monitor API for resource metrics.
components.name: azuremonitorreceiver

components.description: The [Carbon](https://github.com/graphite-project/carbon) Receiver supports Carbon's [plaintext protocol](https://graphite.readthedocs.io/en/stable/feeding-carbon.html#the-plaintext-protocol).
components.name: carbonreceiver

components.description: The Cisco OS Receiver is a modular receiver that collects metrics from Cisco network devices via SSH connections. It supports multiple scrapers for comprehensive network monitoring and provides native OpenTelemetry OTLP metrics generation.
components.name: ciscoosreceiver

components.description: The Cloudflare Receiver allows Cloudflare's [LogPush Jobs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/logs/logpush/) to send logs over HTTPS from the Cloudflare logs aggregation system to an OpenTelemetry collector.
components.name: cloudflarereceiver

components.description: The Cloud Foundry Receiver connects to the RLP (Reverse Log Proxy) Gateway of the Cloud Foundry installation, typically available at the URL `https://log-stream.<cf-system-domain>`.
components.name: cloudfoundryreceiver

components.description: This receiver can receive data exported by the CollectD's `write_http` plugin. Only JSON format is supported. Authentication is not supported at this time.
components.name: collectdreceiver

components.description: This receiver fetches stats from a CouchDB server using the `/_node/{node-name}/_stats/couchdb` [endpoint](https://docs.couchdb.org/en/latest/api/server/common.html#node-node-name-stats).
components.name: couchdbreceiver

components.description: The Datadog receiver enables translation between Datadog and OpenTelemetry-compatible backends. It currently has support for Datadog's APM traces, metrics, and logs.
components.name: datadogreceiver

components.description: The Docker Stats receiver queries the local Docker daemon's container stats API for all desired running containers on a configured interval. These stats are for container resource usage of cpu, memory, network, and the [blkio controller](https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/cgroup-v1/blkio-controller.txt).
components.name: dockerstatsreceiver

components.description: This receiver queries the Elasticsearch [node stats](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/cluster-nodes-stats.html), [cluster health](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/cluster-health.html) and [index stats](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/indices-stats.html) endpoints in order to scrape metrics from a running Elasticsearch cluster.
components.name: elasticsearchreceiver

components.description: This is a receiver for the [Envoy gRPC ALS](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v3/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/v3/als.proto#envoy-v3-api-msg-extensions-access-loggers-grpc-v3-httpgrpcaccesslogconfig) sink.
components.name: envoyalsreceiver

components.description: An Expvar Receiver scrapes metrics from [expvar](https://pkg.go.dev/expvar), which exposes data in JSON format from an HTTP endpoint. The metrics are extracted from the `expvar` variable [memstats](https://pkg.go.dev/runtime#MemStats), which exposes various information about the Go runtime.
components.name: expvarreceiver

components.description: This receiver can receive telemetry data in [Faro format](https://github.com/grafana/faro) that adheres to the [Faro OpenAPI schema](https://github.com/grafana/faro/blob/main/spec/gen/faro.gen.yaml).
components.name: faroreceiver

components.description: This receiver tails and parses logs from files.
components.name: filelogreceiver

components.description: The File Stats receiver collects metrics from files specified with a glob pattern.
components.name: filestatsreceiver

components.description: This receiver uses Flink's [REST API](https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.14/docs/ops/metrics/#rest-api-integration) to collect Jobmanager, Taskmanager, Job, Task and Operator metrics.
components.name: flinkmetricsreceiver

components.description: This receiver runs a TCP server that accepts events via the [Fluent Forward protocol](https://github.com/fluent/fluentd/wiki/Forward-Protocol-Specification-v1).
components.name: fluentforwardreceiver

components.description: The primary objective of the Google Cloud Monitoring Receiver is to gather time series metrics data from all Google services and convert this data into a pipeline format that facilitates further use.
components.name: googlecloudmonitoringreceiver

components.description: This receiver gets OTLP messages from a Google Cloud [Pubsub](https://cloud.google.com/pubsub) subscription.
components.name: googlecloudpubsubreceiver

components.description: The HAProxy receiver generates metrics by polling periodically the HAProxy process through a dedicated socket or HTTP URL.
components.name: haproxyreceiver

components.description: The Host Metrics receiver generates metrics about the host system scraped from various sources and host entity event as log. This is intended to be used when the collector is deployed as an agent.
components.name: hostmetricsreceiver

components.description: The HTTP Check Receiver can be used for synthetic checks against HTTP endpoints.
components.name: httpcheckreceiver

components.description: This receiver contains the implementation of the Huawei Cloud [Cloud Eye Service](https://www.huaweicloud.com/intl/en-us/product/ces.html) (CES) receiver for the OpenTelemetry Collector. The receiver collects metrics from Huawei Cloud's CES service and sends them to the OpenTelemetry Collector for processing and exporting.
components.name: huaweicloudcesreceiver

components.description: The ICMP Check Receiver can be used for synthetic checks against network endpoints. This receiver will send ICMP Echo Requests (pings) to the specified `targets`. It provides device availability monitoring and interface statistics with native OpenTelemetry OTLP metrics generation.
components.name: icmpcheckreceiver

components.description: The Microsoft IIS Receiver grabs metrics about an IIS instance using the Windows Performance Counters. Because of this, it is a Windows only receiver.
components.name: iisreceiver

components.description: This receiver accepts metrics data as [InfluxDB Line Protocol](https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v2.0/reference/syntax/line-protocol/).
components.name: influxdbreceiver

components.description: The Jaeger Receiver receives trace data in the [Jaeger](https://www.jaegertracing.io/) format.
components.name: jaegerreceiver

components.description: The Journald Receiver parses Journald events from systemd journal.
components.name: journaldreceiver

components.description: The Kubernetes Cluster receiver collects cluster-level metrics and entity events from the Kubernetes API server. It uses the K8s API to listen for updates. A single instance of this receiver should be used to monitor a cluster.
components.name: k8sclusterreceiver

components.description: The Kubernetes Events Receiver collects events from the Kubernetes API server. It collects all the new or updated events that come in.
components.name: k8seventsreceiver

components.description: The Kubernetes Objects Receiver collects (pull/watch) objects from the Kubernetes API server.
components.name: k8sobjectsreceiver

components.description: The Kafka Metrics Receiver collects kafka metrics (brokers, topics, partitions, consumer groups) from a kafka server, and converts them into otlp.
components.name: kafkametricsreceiver

components.description: The Kafka Receiver receives telemetry data from Kafka, with configurable topics and encodings. If used in conjunction with the `kafkaexporter` configured with `include_metadata_keys`, The Kafka Receiver will also propagate the Kafka headers to the downstream pipeline, giving access to the rest of the pipeline to arbitrary metadata keys and values.
components.name: kafkareceiver

components.description: The Kubelet Stats Receiver pulls node, pod, container, and volume metrics from the API server on a kubelet and sends it down the metric pipeline for further processing.
components.name: kubeletstatsreceiver

components.description: The Libhoney Receiver will accept Trace and Logs signals that are emitted from applications that are instrumented using [Libhoney](https://docs.honeycomb.io/send-data/logs/structured/libhoney/) libraries.
components.name: libhoneyreceiver

components.description: The Loki receiver implements the [Loki push api](https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/clients/promtail/#loki-push-api) as specified in [Loki HTTP API: Ingest logs](https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/reference/loki-http-api/#ingest-logs). It allows Promtail instances to specify the open telemetry collector as their lokiAddress. This receiver runs HTTP and GRPC servers to ingest log entries in Loki format.
components.name: lokireceiver

components.description: The macOS Unified Logging Receiver collects logs from macOS systems using the native `log` command. This receiver supports both live system logs and archived log files (`.logarchive`).
components.name: macosunifiedloggingreceiver

components.description: This receiver can fetch stats from a Memcached instance using the [stats command](https://github.com/memcached/memcached/wiki/Commands#statistics). A detailed description of all the stats available is at https://github.com/memcached/memcached/blob/master/doc/protocol.txt#L1159.
components.name: memcachedreceiver

components.description: The MongoDB Atlas Receiver receives metrics from [MongoDB Atlas](https://www.mongodb.com/cloud/atlas) via their [monitoring APIs](https://docs.atlas.mongodb.com/reference/api/monitoring-and-logs/), as well as alerts via a configured [webhook](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/tutorial/third-party-service-integrations/) and events from [events APIs](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/reference/api/events/).
components.name: mongodbatlasreceiver

components.description: This receiver fetches stats from a MongoDB instance using the [golang mongo driver](https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver). Stats are collected via MongoDB's `dbStats` and `serverStatus` commands.
components.name: mongodbreceiver

components.description: This receiver supports opening a Unix Named Pipe (aka FIFO), and reading logs from it.
components.name: namedpipereceiver

components.description: The Netflow Receiver can listen for [netflow](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NetFlow), [sflow](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SFlow), and [ipfix](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IP_Flow_Information_Export) data and convert it to OpenTelemetry logs. The receiver is based on the [goflow2](https://github.com/netsampler/goflow2) project.
components.name: netflowreceiver

components.description: This receiver can fetch stats from a NGINX instance using the `ngx_http_stub_status_module` module's `status` endpoint.
components.name: nginxreceiver

components.description: This receiver fetches metrics important to run virtual networking using NSX-T. The receiver ingests metrics via the [NSX Rest API](https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-NSX-Data-Center-for-vSphere/6.4/nsx_64_api.pdf).
components.name: nsxtreceiver

components.description: This receiver periodically retrieves the clock offset from a NTP server.
components.name: ntpreceiver

components.description: This receiver periodically queries an Oracle Database host to collect metrics.
components.name: oracledbreceiver

components.description: The osquery Receiver runs queries run on an [osquery](https://osquery.io/)'s daemon on a schedule and converts the output to logs.
components.name: osqueryreceiver

components.description: Receives telemetry data using [OpenTelemetry Protocol with Apache Arrow](https://github.com/open-telemetry/otel-arrow) and standard [OTLP]( https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/protocol/otlp.md) protocol via gRPC.
components.name: otelarrowreceiver

components.description: This receiver will read pipeline data from JSON files. The data is written in [Protobuf JSON encoding](https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/proto3#json) using [OpenTelemetry protocol](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-proto).
components.name: otlpjsonfilereceiver

components.description: The Podman Stats Receiver queries the Podman service API to fetch stats for all running containers on a configured interval. These stats are for container resource usage of cpu, memory, network, and the [blkio controller](https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/cgroup-v1/blkio-controller.txt).
components.name: podmanreceiver

components.description: This receiver queries the PostgreSQL [statistics collector](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/13/monitoring-stats.html).
components.name: postgresqlreceiver

components.description: The Pprof Receiver collects profiles from files specified with a glob pattern, from a remote endpoint or the running collector.
components.name: pprofreceiver

components.description: The Prometheus Receiver receives metric data in [Prometheus](https://prometheus.io/) format.
components.name: prometheusreceiver

components.description: This receiver implements the [Prometheus Remote Write 2.0 protocol](https://prometheus.io/docs/specs/prw/remote_write_spec_2_0/).
components.name: prometheusremotewritereceiver

components.description: The Pulsar Receiver receives logs, metrics, and traces from [Apache Pulsar](https://pulsar.apache.org/).
components.name: pulsarreceiver

components.description: The Pure Storage FlashArray Receiver receives metrics from the Pure Storage FlashArray.
components.name: purefareceiver

components.description: The Pure Storage FlashBlade receiver, receives metrics from Pure Storage FlashBlade via the [Pure Storage FlashBlade OpenMetrics Exporter](https://github.com/PureStorage-OpenConnect/pure-fb-openmetrics-exporter)
components.name: purefbreceiver

components.description: This receiver fetches stats from a RabbitMQ node using the [RabbitMQ Management Plugin](https://www.rabbitmq.com/management.html).
components.name: rabbitmqreceiver

components.description: This receiver can instantiate other receivers at runtime based on whether observed endpoints match a configured rule.
components.name: receivercreator

components.description: This receiver fetches metrics for a server running a [Redfish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Redfish_(specification)) API.
components.name: redfishreceiver

components.description: The Redis Receiver is designed to retrieve Redis INFO data from a single Redis instance, build metrics from that data, and send them to the next consumer at a configurable interval.
components.name: redisreceiver

components.description: This receiver can fetch stats from a SAP HANA instance. It leverages the [driver](https://github.com/SAP/go-hdb) written by SAP for connecting to SAP HANA with the golang sql module to execute several monitoring queries.
components.name: saphanareceiver

components.description: This receiver provides a simple configuration interface to configure the prometheus receiver to scrape metrics from a single target.
components.name: simpleprometheusreceiver

components.description: Receives trace data and metric data in [SkyWalking](https://skywalking.apache.org/) format.
components.name: skywalkingreceiver

components.description: This receiver fetches stats from an SNMP enabled host using a [golang snmp client](https://github.com/gosnmp/gosnmp). Metrics are collected based upon different configurations in the config file.
components.name: snmpreceiver

components.description: This receiver collects metrics from a Snowflake account by connecting to and querying a Snowflake deployment.
components.name: snowflakereceiver

components.description: The Solace Receiver receives trace data from a [Solace PubSub+ Event Broker](https://solace.com/products/event-broker/).
components.name: solacereceiver

components.description: The Splunk Enterprise Receiver is a pull based tool which enables the ingestion of performance metrics describing the operational status of a user's Splunk Enterprise deployment to an appropriate observability tool. It is designed to leverage several different data sources to gather these metrics including the [introspection api endpoint](https://help.splunk.com/en/splunk-enterprise/leverage-rest-apis/rest-api-reference/9.1/introspection-endpoints/introspection-endpoint-descriptions) and serializing results from ad-hoc searches. Because of this, care must be taken by users when enabling metrics as running searches can effect your Splunk Enterprise Deployment and introspection may fail to report for Splunk Cloud deployments. The primary purpose of this receiver is to empower those tasked with the maintenance and care of a Splunk Enterprise deployment to leverage opentelemetry and their observability toolset in their jobs.
components.name: splunkenterprisereceiver

components.description: The Splunk HEC receiver accepts events in the [Splunk HEC format](https://help.splunk.com/en/splunk-enterprise/get-data-in/get-started-with-getting-data-in/10.0/get-data-with-http-event-collector/format-events-for-http-event-collector). This allows the collector to receive logs and metrics. The collector accepts data formatted as JSON [HEC events](https://help.splunk.com/en/splunk-enterprise/get-data-in/get-started-with-getting-data-in/10.0/get-data-with-http-event-collector/format-events-for-http-event-collector#ariaid-title5) under any path or as EOL separated log [raw data](https://help.splunk.com/en/splunk-enterprise/get-data-in/get-started-with-getting-data-in/10.0/get-data-with-http-event-collector/format-events-for-http-event-collector#raw-event-parsing-0) if sent to the `raw_path` path.
components.name: splunkhecreceiver

components.description: The SQL Query Receiver uses custom SQL queries to generate logs and/or metrics from a database connection.
components.name: sqlqueryreceiver

components.description: The `sqlserver` receiver grabs metrics/logs about a Microsoft SQL Server instance. The receiver works by either using the Windows Performance Counters, or by directly connecting to the instance and querying it. Windows Performance Counters are only available when running on Windows.
components.name: sqlserverreceiver

components.description: This receiver creates stats by connecting to an SSH server which may be an SFTP server.
components.name: sshcheckreceiver

components.description: StatsD receiver for ingesting StatsD messages(https://github.com/statsd/statsd/blob/master/docs/metric_types.md) into the OpenTelemetry Collector. Note: This receiver does not support horizontally scaled collector deployments. It is intended to run in agent mode, where a single collector instance receives StatsD input.
components.name: statsdreceiver

components.description: Receives data via gRPC in OTel/STEF format.
components.name: stefreceiver

components.description: Parses Syslogs received over TCP or UDP.
components.name: syslogreceiver

components.description: The Systemd Receiver gathers metrics for locally running systemd units.
components.name: systemdreceiver

components.description: This receiver creates stats by connecting to a TCP server.
components.name: tcpcheckreceiver

components.description: The TCP Log Receiver receives logs over TCP.
components.name: tcplogreceiver

components.description: This receiver emits metrics about x.509 certificates.
components.name: tlscheckreceiver

components.description: The UDP Log Receiver receives logs over UDP.
components.name: udplogreceiver

components.description: This receiver fetches metrics from a vCenter or ESXi host running VMware vSphere APIs.
components.name: vcenterreceiver

components.description: The OpenTelemetry **Verbatim Capture & Replay (VCR) toolset** enables full-fidelity capture and replay of all telemetry data stored on disk. It replays previously captured telemetry while **preserving original event timestamps**, allowing you to simulate realistic traffic timing independent of when the data was captured.
components.name: vcrreceiver

components.description: The Webhook Event Receiver is a push based event collector component meant to act as a generally available receiver for any webhook style data source. It is designed to work alongside other pipeline components like the [transform processor](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/tree/main/processor/transformprocessor) to allow the ingestion of generic events as logs into the opentelemetry collector.
components.name: webhookeventreceiver

components.description: This receiver tails and parses logs from windows event log API using the [opentelemetry-log-collection](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-log-collection) library.
components.name: windowseventlogreceiver

components.description: This receiver, for Windows only, captures the configured system, application, or custom performance counter data from the Windows registry using the [PDH interface](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/perfctrs/using-the-pdh-functions-to-consume-counter-data).
components.name: windowsperfcountersreceiver

components.description: The Windows Service Receiver is a receiver for scraping information about the state of services running on a Windows machine.
components.name: windowsservicereceiver

components.description: The YANG/gRPC receiver receives metrics offered using the [YANG (Yet Another New Generation) data model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/YANG), expressed over [gRPC](https://ciscolearning.github.io/cisco-learning-codelabs/posts/yangsuite-restconf/#0).
components.name: yanggrpcreceiver

components.description: This receiver receives spans from [Zipkin](https://zipkin.io/) (V1 and V2).
components.name: zipkinreceiver

components.description: The Zookeeper Receiver collects metrics from a Zookeeper instance, using the `mntr` command. The `mntr` 4 letter word command needs to be enabled for the receiver to be able to collect metrics.
components.name: zookeeperreceiver
